name: react
context: Build React components: hooks, context, server components, and common patterns
info: This skill covers building React components using modern patterns: functional components with JSX/TSX, hooks for state and effects, context for dependency injection, server components for streaming SSR, and established patterns like compound components, render props, and higher-order components. React 19 introduces server components, use() hook, and Actions for form handling. All examples use TypeScript. Components should be accessible by default — see [[frontend/accessibility]] for ARIA and keyboard patterns.

sections.title: Components and JSX
sections.body: Components are functions returning JSX. Type props with TypeScript interfaces. Use named exports for tree-shaking (see [[frontend/bundling#tree-shaking]]). Prefer composition over inheritance. Keep components small and focused on a single responsibility.

sections.title: React hooks
sections.body: useState for local state, useEffect for side effects, useRef for DOM refs and mutable values, useMemo/useCallback for memoization, useContext for reading context. React 19 adds use() for unwrapping promises in render. Custom hooks (use*) extract reusable stateful logic. See [[frontend/state#local-state]] for state management guidance.

sections.title: React context
sections.body: createContext + Provider for cross-cutting concerns (theme, auth, locale, i18n). Wrap useContext in a custom hook for cleaner API. Avoid overusing context for frequently-changing state — use [[frontend/state#zustand]] instead. Context re-renders all consumers on change.

sections.title: Server components
sections.body: React Server Components (RSC) run on the server, can access databases and APIs directly, and stream HTML to the client. Default in Next.js App Router. Add 'use client' only when you need browser APIs, event handlers, or client hooks. RSC reduce client bundle size (see [[frontend/bundling#tree-shaking]]).

sections.title: Common patterns
sections.body: Compound components for parent-child context sharing (Tabs, Accordion). Render props for flexible rendering. Custom hooks for reusable logic. Controlled inputs bind to state, uncontrolled use refs. Error boundaries catch render errors. Style with [[frontend/css]], manage state with [[frontend/state]], test with [[frontend/testing]].
